---
title: "R Notebook"
output: html_notebook
---


```{r}
library(boot)
library(DAAG)
library(lme4)

library(tidyverse)
library(caret)


library(plyr)
library(caret)
library(car)
library(pROC)
library(readxl)

library(optimx)
```


```{r}
mydata <- read_excel("./fractions2013.xlsx")
names(mydata)  
```



```{r}
mydata$`First Attempt` = revalue(mydata$`First Attempt`, c("incorrect"= 0))
mydata$`First Attempt` = revalue(mydata$`First Attempt`, c("hint"= 0))
mydata$`First Attempt` = revalue(mydata$`First Attempt`, c("correct"= 1))
  
mydata["Outcome"] = as.numeric(mydata$`First Attempt`)

mydata <- na.omit(mydata)
  
```


```{r}
 mydata["KCModel"] = mydata$`KC (Default)`
  mydata["OpportunityModel"] = as.numeric(as.character(mydata$`Opportunity`))
  
  
  #Instead of Opportunities we need the number of correct and incorrect answers for PFM
mydata["CorrectModel"] = as.numeric(as.character(mydata$`Corrects`))
mydata["IncorrectModel"] = as.numeric(as.character(mydata$`Incorrects`))

#For IFM we additionally need the number of tells
mydata["TellsModel"] = as.numeric(as.character(mydata$`Hints`))
  
```


```{r}
AFM_form <- Outcome ~ AnonStudentId +KCModel + KCModel:OpportunityModel
 AFMTrainingModel <- glm(AFM_form, family=binomial(), data= mydata); 
summary(AFMTrainingModel)
 
 summary(AFMTrainingModel)$aic	

 
 #This is the formula for the PFM model
#Instead of the opportunities model I put the models for the correct and incorrect answers here
PFM_form <- Outcome ~ AnonStudentId +KCModel + KCModel:(CorrectModel + IncorrectModel)
 PFMTrainingModel <- glm(PFM_form, family=binomial(), data= mydata); 
print('---------------------- PFM model ---------------------------')
summary(PFMTrainingModel)
 
 summary(PFMTrainingModel)$aic	

#This is the formula for the IFM model
#Instead of the opportunities model I put the models for the correct and incorrect answers as well as the number of tells here
IFM_form <- Outcome ~ AnonStudentId +KCModel + KCModel:(CorrectModel + IncorrectModel + TellsModel)
 IFMTrainingModel <- glm(IFM_form, family=binomial(), data= mydata); 
print('---------------------- IFM model ---------------------------')
summary(IFMTrainingModel)
 
 summary(IFMTrainingModel)$aic
 
 
  
```


```{r}
#when you execture that code at home, select as many entries as the combination of day and month of birthday.
#for example, if your birthday is on 12th of July, you can choose, 127 entries
i= sample.int(nrow(mydata), 127)

TrainingDataTemp  <- mydata[-i,];
TestingDataTemp  <- mydata[i,];


#retrain model

AFMTrainingModel <- glm(AFM_form, family=binomial(), data= TrainingDataTemp); 

#predict for testset
probabilities <- AFMTrainingModel %>% predict(TestingDataTemp, type = "response")
head(probabilities)

#lets say that we set the classification threshold to 0.5
# probability >0.5 means the student will answer correctly
# probability <0.5 means the student will answer incorrectly

TestingDataTemp$prediction <- ifelse(probabilities >0.5,1,0)

#Check the error between real outcomes and predicted variables 
sqrt(mean((TestingDataTemp$Outcome - TestingDataTemp$prediction)^2))


#Training and test set can be the same for PFM and IFM
i= sample.int(nrow(mydata), 3112)

TrainingDataTemp  <- mydata[-i,];
TestingDataTemp  <- mydata[i,];


#retrain both models

PFMTrainingModel <- glm(PFM_form, family=binomial(), data= TrainingDataTemp); 
IFMTrainingModel <- glm(IFM_form, family=binomial(), data= TrainingDataTemp); 


#Predictions and classification for PFM
pfmProbabilities <- PFMTrainingModel %>% predict(TestingDataTemp, type = "response")
print('---------------------- PFM results ---------------------------')
head(pfmProbabilities)

#lets say that we set the classification threshold to 0.5
# probability >0.5 means the student will answer correctly
# probability <0.5 means the student will answer incorrectly
TestingDataTemp$pfmPrediction <- ifelse(pfmProbabilities >0.5,1,0)

#Check the error between real outcomes and predicted variables 
sqrt(mean((TestingDataTemp$Outcome - TestingDataTemp$pfmPrediction)^2))


#Predictions and classification for IFM
ifmProbabilities <- IFMTrainingModel %>% predict(TestingDataTemp, type = "response")
print('---------------------- IFM results ---------------------------')
head(ifmProbabilities)

#lets say that we set the classification threshold to 0.5
# probability >0.5 means the student will answer correctly
# probability <0.5 means the student will answer incorrectly
TestingDataTemp$ifmPrediction <- ifelse(ifmProbabilities >0.5,1,0)

#Check the error between real outcomes and predicted variables 
sqrt(mean((TestingDataTemp$Outcome - TestingDataTemp$ifmPrediction)^2))

```
